screens.name: Audio-to-score artifact workflow
screens.purpose: Define the ordered evidence, adjudication, transcription, arrangement, notation, and review surfaces consumed by Codex.
screens.primary_action: Advance only when the required upstream artifact and approval state exist.

screens.name: Repository skill synchronization command
screens.purpose: Check or deploy one explicit repository-owned skill without touching unrelated installed skills.
screens.primary_action: Check or install an explicitly named skill.

ux_rules: Use neutral structural labels until semantic form labels have evidence.
Allow evidence extraction before form approval, but prohibit notation-ready melody or harmony decisions until form is approved.
Keep the linear performance timeline authoritative and derive compressed written form from explicit mappings.
Keep observed source transcription and generated accompaniment in separate provenance layers.
Require explicit human decisions when form, transcription, or arrangement confidence falls below the contract threshold.
Make skill synchronization check-only by default in verification and require an explicit install mode for mutation.
human_checkpoints: Approve or correct the frozen song form before notation-ready transcription.
Approve low-confidence melody, bass, harmony, and lyric alignment before the linear score is final.
Approve generated accompaniment separately from source transcription.
Review detected drift before overwriting an installed skill that contains direct local edits.

screen_contexts.layout_intent: Keep the core procedure concise while routing concrete schemas and domain checks to one-level references.
interaction_risks: Finalizing form before melody and harmony evidence can challenge it.
Allowing chord analysis to redefine approved form silently.
Mixing generated arrangement notes with observed source notes.
Compressing repeats before the linear timeline is verified.
notes: No rendered product surface is changed.

screen_contexts.layout_intent: Use explicit positional arguments and short actionable diagnostics.
interaction_risks: Path traversal outside the repository skill root.
Partial installation after an I/O failure.
Treating the receipt file as repository content drift.

constraints: Keep SKILL.md below 500 lines and move detailed schemas into directly linked references.
Do not claim that signal extraction tools provide authoritative musical answers.
Require stable IDs and source references across form, melody, harmony, and score mappings.
Do not require every measure to contain a chord; rests, tacet, and unresolved harmony must remain representable.
open_decisions: Exact signal-extraction model selection remains implementation-specific evidence tooling.
Exact piano or guitar accompaniment vocabulary remains user-configurable.

constraints: Require a lowercase skill name matching the repository directory name.
Resolve and verify both source and target remain under their expected skill roots.
Stage the full skill before replacing the exact installed target.
Never enumerate and replace multiple skills implicitly.
Exclude only the generated receipt from drift comparison.

critical_judgments: Parallel evidence extraction and form-first adjudication are not conflated.
Preliminary and frozen form are distinct.
Linear and written-form scores have explicit bidirectional mappings.
Source transcription and accompaniment arrangement cannot share provenance.
must_not_regress: Pickup, meter change, repeated occurrence, and altered repeat handling remain supported.

critical_judgments: Check mode has no filesystem writes.
Install changes only one exact target.
A failed replacement restores the previous installed copy.

visual_constraints: Not applicable: this task changes skills, contracts, validation, and a command-line synchronization workflow only.
